script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. Another day, another coffee.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。また一日が始まるし、またコーヒーですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Morning, Alex. Still relying on that, huh?
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。まだそれに頼ってるの？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, definitely. My kid was up at 4 AM today. July 18th, 2026, and I'm already tired.
script.ja: ええ、もちろんです。うちの子、今日は午前4時に起きたんです。2026年7月18日、もう疲れてますよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Rough start. Looks like it's going to be partly cloudy later. High of 30.2, low of 24.4. And there's a 39% chance of rain.
script.ja: 大変な始まりね。午後は部分的に曇りになりそうよ。最高気温30.2度、最低24.4度。降水確率は39%だって。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Hmm, still hot enough. I hate that sticky heat.
script.ja: うーん、まだ十分暑いですね。あのべたつく暑さ、嫌だな。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yeah. Well, did you see the news about rice flour? The government wants people to use it more for sweets because rice consumption is down.
script.ja: ええ、そうね。ところで、米粉のニュース見た？政府が、お米の消費が減ってるから、お菓子にもっと使うように奨励してるんだって。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, that. I feel like I read this every year. It's not going to change at my office, though. Still bentos every day.
script.ja: ああ、それですか。毎年読んでる気がしますね。うちの会社では変わらないでしょうけどね。毎日お弁当だし。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Right. It's tough to shift habits. I wonder if it will really help the farmers.
script.ja: そうね。習慣を変えるのは難しいわ。本当に農家のためになるのかしら。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I hope so. But talking about work, I had an `impromptu` meeting with my boss yesterday. Just popped into my office.
script.ja: そうだといいんですけど。でも仕事の話だと、昨日、上司と急に`impromptu`なミーティングがあったんですよ。突然私のオフィスに来て。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: An `impromptu` meeting? That just means it was unplanned. No notice.
script.ja: `impromptu`なミーティング？それはただ、計画されてなかったってことよ。事前連絡なしで。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, unplanned. Got it. So, no time to prepare. Anyway, what else is happening? Oh, that survey about foreigners wanting to work here long-term dropping.
script.ja: ああ、計画されてなかった、と。なるほど。だから準備する時間もなかったわけですね。ところで、他には何かありますか？あ、日本で長期的に働きたい外国人が減ってるっていう調査。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, I saw that. Japan is popular for tourists, but not so much for working long-term, apparently.
script.ja: ええ、それ見たわ。日本は観光客には人気だけど、長期で働くにはそうでもないみたいね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It's surprising. Everyone wants to visit, but not live? Is it the pay, or the work culture?
script.ja: 意外ですね。みんな訪れたいけど、住むのは違うってことですか？給料の問題ですか、それとも労働文化？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Probably a mix. My son sometimes talks about working abroad when he's older. He says he wants more flexibility.
script.ja: 多分、両方よ。うちの息子も、もっと大きくなったら海外で働きたいって言うことがあるわ。もっと柔軟性が欲しいって。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Flexibility... yeah. I can see that. It's tough sometimes, trying to balance work and home life. Especially with a baby.
script.ja: 柔軟性…そうですね。わかります。仕事と家庭生活のバランスを取るのが大変な時がありますね。特に赤ちゃんがいると。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. But you're doing well. Kids are pretty `resilient`, even when they're little.
script.ja: そうね。でもあなたはよくやってるわ。子どもって、小さい頃でもかなり`resilient`よ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: `Resilient`? What does that mean exactly?
script.ja: `Resilient`？それって具体的にどういう意味ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It means they bounce back quickly from difficulties. Like when they fall down, they cry for a minute, then they're off playing again.
script.ja: 困難からすぐに立ち直るってことよ。転んだ時みたいに、一分泣いて、また遊びに戻るでしょ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, that makes sense. My kid does that. Falls over, then just laughs. Amazing, actually.
script.ja: ああ、なるほど。うちの子もそうですよ。転んで、そのまま笑ってる。すごいですよね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They adapt quickly. That's a good thing. Speaking of adapting, did you hear about the 'Roppongi Kabuki Part II Zatoichi'?
script.ja: すぐに順応するのよ。それはいいことだわ。順応といえば、「六本木歌舞伎 第2弾 座頭市」のニュース聞いた？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Roppongi Kabuki? No. Sounds... modern?
script.ja: 六本木歌舞伎？いいえ。なんだか…モダンですね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They're calling it the most accessible Kabuki ever. Only a few hours, and apparently, it's easier to understand.
script.ja: これまでで一番とっつきやすい歌舞伎だそうよ。数時間で終わるし、どうやら理解しやすいらしいわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's kind of amazing, actually. Maybe I should take my wife. It's The Daily Tanu-chan Show, and we're always looking for new things to try.
script.ja: それはちょっとすごいですね、実際。妻を連れて行ってみようかな。ザ・デイリータヌちゃんショーですが、私たちはいつも新しいことに挑戦する機会を探しているんですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Could be fun. My daughter might even be interested. She likes new things.
script.ja: 楽しいかもしれないわね。うちの娘も興味を持つかも。新しいものが好きだから。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Your daughter is into traditional performing arts?
script.ja: 娘さんは伝統芸能に興味があるんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Not really, but if it's new and different, she'll give it a try. She helped me with dinner last weekend. We made a big batch of chicken adobo.
script.ja: そうでもないんだけど、新しくて変わってるなら試してみるわね。先週末、夕食を手伝ってくれたのよ。鶏肉のアドボをたくさん作ったの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Chicken adobo? Sounds good. For meal prep?
script.ja: チキンアドボ？いいですね。作り置きですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly. Saves a lot of time during the week. That's my little secret for sanity.
script.ja: その通り。平日の時間をかなり節約できるわ。それが私の正気を保つ秘訣ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I should try that. With a baby, any time saved is a victory.
script.ja: 私も試してみようかな。赤ちゃんがいると、どんな時間の節約でも勝利ですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It really is. Oh, and today is Women's Minister Day. On this day in 1960, Masa Nakayama became Japan's first female cabinet minister.
script.ja: 本当にそうね。そうそう、今日は女性大臣の日よ。1960年の今日、中山マサさんが厚生大臣として入閣して、日本初の女性大臣が誕生したの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, I didn't know that. That's a long time ago. 1960...
script.ja: へえ、知りませんでした。ずいぶん昔ですね。1960年…

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes. It's good to remember milestones like that. It took a long time, but it was an important step.
script.ja: ええ。そういう節目を覚えておくのはいいことよ。時間はかかったけど、重要な一歩だったわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Definitely. Hopefully, we'll see more women in leadership roles soon. Well, thanks for the chat, Sarah. I should get my second coffee.
script.ja: 確かに。もっと早く女性が指導的役割に就くのを見たいですね。では、サラさん、お話しありがとうございました。2杯目のコーヒーを淹れてきます。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You do that, Alex. See you later.
script.ja: そうしなさい、アレックス。また後でね。

news.summary: With the decline of rice consumption in Japan, the farm ministry is encouraging the use of rice flour to make sweets as a way of expanding how the…

news.summary: These days, Japan is more popular than ever with international tourists, and that trend doesn’t show signs of slowing down anytime soon. However, the recently released results of…

news.title: ‘Roppongi Kabuki Part II Zatoichi’ – the most accessible Kabuki ever
news.summary: Great news! You don't have to sit down for four hours without having a clue as to what is going on watching Kabuki, one of Japan’s must-see traditional…

wikipedia.title: 女性大臣の日
wikipedia.description: 1960（昭和35）年、第一次池田勇人内閣に中山マサが厚生大臣として入閣し、日本初の女性大臣が誕生した。
